title: audit — HTTP middleware
description: One owned middleware — `audit.enforce_read_only` — used on every /audits* route as defense-in-depth against a rogue route registration attempting to expose non-GET methods. Refuses PATCH / POST / PUT / DELETE with 405 Method Not Allowed.

middleware.purpose: Belt-and-braces refusal of non-GET methods on any /audits* route. AuditPolicy already refuses create/update/delete at the authorization layer; this middleware refuses at the request-parsing layer BEFORE authorization runs. Prevents any future rogue route registration from accidentally exposing a write path.
steps: IF method === 'GET' OR method === 'HEAD' OR method === 'OPTIONS': next().

ELSE: return 405 Method Not Allowed with response body { code: 'NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_ROUTE_READ_ONLY', message: 'Audit routes are read-only. Capture is via the vendor Auditable trait; there is no HTTP write path.' }.
Write a security audit row logging the refused request (event=security.audit_write_attempt_refused, actor from request context, path from request URI, method from request).
middleware.notes: Skips the enforcement for POST /audits/export + POST /audit-retention-policies + PATCH/DELETE /audit-retention-policies/{policy} — those legitimately need non-GET methods. The enforcement targets the audit ROW routes (/audits + /audits/{audit} + /audits/by-*), not the retention-policy CRUD or the async export request route.
middleware.config_toggle: config.audit.immutability.enforce_read_only_middleware (true by default; can be off in test env)

entitlements.check.reason: Universal entitlement gate. Applied on every route via `entitlements.check:audit_capture` etc.

audit.enforce_capture_kill_switch.reason: Capture is switched off at the ObserverUnbindingListener level (fires when audit_capture entitlement is revoked OR the audit.capture feature flag is off). No middleware needed — the vendor observer is never even called.
